{"parentUuid":"e7200379-6f78-4107-ab7d-d0c0ad40416f","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"816adb34-9916-4b87-bdb8-a5cdf8441b9c","agentId":"ac5270b7f24ffa85e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Evals & Quality Framework — Design Spec
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-03-12
     4→**Status**: Draft
     5→**Priority**: P2 — Quality infrastructure
     6→**Research**: `docs/specs/evals-research.md`
     7→
     8→## Problem
     9→
    10→Founder OS has 32 command namespaces producing LLM-generated outputs (email drafts, meeting briefs, invoices, reports). There is no systematic way to measure output quality, detect regressions, or feed quality signals back into the learning cycle. The existing intelligence engine tracks pattern confidence but doesn't evaluate whether outputs actually satisfy the founder's needs.
    11→
    12→Without quality measurement, the system can't answer: "Is my AI assistant getting better or worse?"
    13→
    14→## Solution
    15→
    16→A three-layer eval framework embedded as a sub-module of the existing intelligence engine. Deterministic checks run synchronously via a Node.js runner. LLM-based scoring runs asynchronously via a background Haiku agent. Results feed into the existing pattern lifecycle. Founders see nothing unless quality regresses — then they get a warning.
    17→
    18→**Key design principles:**
    19→- Zero founder configuration — evals work automatically after plugin install
    20→- Zero session overhead — LLM scoring runs in background, never blocks the founder
    21→- Programmatic where possible — Tier 0 and Tier 1 are pure Node.js, not LLM-interpreted
    22→- Silent by default — regression alerts only, no inline badges or scores
    23→- Minimal schema — 4 tables, add more only when proven necessary
    24→
    25→## Design
    26→
    27→### Architecture Overview
    28→
    29→```
    30→EVAL PIPELINE
    31→=============
    32→
    33→Founder runs /founder-os:namespace:command
    34→                    │
    35→                    ▼
    36→         ┌──────────────────┐
    37→         │  COMMAND EXECUTES │
    38→         │  (normal flow)    │
    39→         └────────┬─────────┘
    40→                  │ output generated
    41→                  ▼
    42→         ┌──────────────────┐
    43→         │  post-task HOOK   │
    44→         │  fires            │
    45→         └────────┬─────────┘
    46→                  │
    47→                  ▼
    48→    ┌─────────────────────────────┐
    49→    │  NODE.JS EVAL RUNNER        │
    50→    │  (synchronous, <50ms)       │
    51→    │                             │
    52→    │  Tier 0: Telemetry          │
    53→    │  • Token count, duration    │
    54→    │  • SHA-256 input/output     │
    55→    │  • INSERT into exec_log     │
    56→    │                             │
    57→    │  Tier 1: Format Checks      │
    58→    │  • Required sections        │
    59→    │  • Word count bounds        │
    60→    │  • Forbidden patterns       │
    61→    │  • UPDATE exec_log.t1_pass  │
    62→    │                             │
    63→    │  Sampling Decision          │
    64→    │  • T1 fail → always Tier 2  │
    65→    │  • Otherwise → 20% random   │
    66→    └──────────┬──────────────────┘
    67→               │
    68→        ┌──────┴──────┐
    69→   NOT SAMPLED     SAMPLED
    70→        │              │
    71→        ▼              ▼
    72→   exit 0          SPAWN eval-judge
    73→   (done)          AGENT (background)
    74→                   model: haiku
    75→                        │
    76→                        ▼
    77→                   ┌─────────────────┐
    78→                   │  EVAL JUDGE      │
    79→                   │  (async, ~500ms) │
    80→                   │                  │
    81→                   │  Per dimension:  │
    82→                   │  • Read criterion│
    83→                   │  • YES/NO judge  │
    84→                   │  • 1-line reason │
    85→                   │                  │
    86→                   │  Write results   │
    87→                   │  to eval_results │
    88→                   │  Update EWMA     │
    89→                   └─────────────────┘
    90→
    91→REGRESSION ALERTING (separate flow)
    92→====================================
    93→
    94→Founder runs any command
    95→        │
    96→        ▼
    97→   pre-task HOOK fires
    98→        │
    99→        ▼
   100→   node eval-runner.mjs --check-regression
   101→        │
   102→        ▼
   103→   Query EWMA for namespace (~1ms)
   104→        │
   105→   ├── EWMA ≥ 0.65 → silent (exit 0)
   106→   └── EWMA < 0.65 → print warning:
   107→       "⚠️ Quality for {ns} commands has dropped
   108→        recently. Run /founder-os:intel:health"
   109→       (once per session per namespace)
   110→```
   111→
   112→### Component 1: Deterministic Runner
   113→
   114→**Location:** `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/`
   115→
   116→```
   117→_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/
   118→├── eval-runner.mjs          — Main entry, called by post-task hook
   119→├── checks/
   120→│   ├── telemetry.mjs        — Tier 0: token count, duration, hashes
   121→│   ├── format.mjs           — Tier 1: sections, word count, forbidden patterns
   122→│   └── schema.mjs           — Tier 1: JSON/structure validation
   123→├── rubrics/
   124→│   ├── universal.json       — Default 6-dimension rubric
   125→│   └── overrides/           — Per-namespace rubric overrides
   126→│       ├── inbox.json
   127→│       ├── invoice.json
   128→│       ├── prep.json
   129→│       └── (added per namespace as needed)
   130→├── db.mjs                   — SQLite read/write, migration checks
   131→└── sampler.mjs              — Sampling decision logic
   132→```
   133→
   134→**Entry point:** `eval-runner.mjs` accepts CLI arguments:
   135→
   136→```bash
   137→# Post-task eval mode
   138→node eval-runner.mjs \
   139→  --namespace inbox \
   140→  --command triage \
   141→  --output-file /tmp/fos-exec-{id}.txt \
   142→  --tokens-in 1200 \
   143→  --tokens-out 800 \
   144→  --duration-ms 3400
   145→
   146→# Regression check mode
   147→node eval-runner.mjs --check-regression --namespace inbox
   148→```
   149→
   150→**Rubric format (JSON, not markdown):**
   151→
   152→```json
   153→{
   154→  "namespace": "inbox",
   155→  "tier1": {
   156→    "required_sections": ["Subject:", "Body:"],
   157→    "min_words": 50,
   158→    "max_words": 500,
   159→    "forbidden_patterns": ["[PLACEHOLDER]", "INSERT_NAME", "{name}"],
   160→    "required_json_fields": null
   161→  },
   162→  "tier2": {
   163→    "sample_rate": 0.20,
   164→    "dimensions": ["accuracy", "tone", "actionability"],
   165→    "weights": {
   166→      "accuracy": 0.25,
   167→      "tone": 0.15,
   168→      "actionability": 0.20
   169→    },
   170→    "criteria": {
   171→      "accuracy": "No fabricated data or claims not present in source",
   172→      "tone": "Professional, matches founder voice profile",
   173→      "actionability": "Includes concrete next step the founder can take"
   174→    },
   175→    "threshold": 0.70
   176→  }
   177→}
   178→```
   179→
   180→**Rubric merging rules:**
   181→- Namespace overrides merge on top of `universal.json`
   182→- If no override exists for a namespace, universal rubric applies (both Tier 1 and Tier 2)
   183→- `universal.json` defines baseline Tier 1 checks (non-empty output, min 10 words, max 5000 words) and all 6 Tier 2 dimensions with default weights
   184→- Namespace overrides can: add Tier 1 checks (e.g., required sections), adjust Tier 2 dimension weights, override criteria text, change sample rate
   185→- Override weights replace universal weights for specified dimensions; unspecified dimensions keep universal weights; all weights are re-normalized to sum to 1.0
   186→
   187→**Dependencies:** `better-sqlite3` (added to plugin `package.json`), `crypto` (built-in Node.js). No other runtime dependencies.
   188→
   189→### Component 2: Eval Judge Agent
   190→
   191→**Location:** `agents/intel/eval-judge.md`
   192→
   193→**Configuration:**
   194→- Model: `haiku` (Boolean rubric scoring is low-complexity)
   195→- Execution: `run_in_background: true` (non-blocking)
   196→- Timeout: 30 seconds
   197→
   198→**Agent behavior:**
   199→1. Receives: command output text, rubric criteria, execution context, and the `intelligence.db` file path
   200→2. For each dimension in the rubric: evaluates YES/NO with one-sentence reasoning
   201→3. Computes weighted overall score across all evaluated dimensions
   202→4. Writes results directly to `eval_results` table via `db.mjs` (the agent calls `node db.mjs --write-eval` as its final step)
   203→
   204→**Write path:** The agent is responsible for persisting its own results. It receives the database path as part of its spawn context and calls the eval runner's `db.mjs` module to INSERT rows into `eval_results` and update the EWMA. This avoids the anti-pattern of the hook trying to collect results from an async process it no longer controls.
   205→
   206→**Output format (all 6 universal dimensions evaluated, namespace overrides adjust weights):**
   207→
   208→```json
   209→{
   210→  "execution_id": "exec-abc123",
   211→  "scores": {
   212→    "completeness": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.20, "reasoning": "All requested sections present" },
   213→    "accuracy": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.25, "reasoning": "All claims supported by source email data" },
   214→    "tone": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.15, "reasoning": "Professional register, appropriate for client context" },
   215→    "actionability": { "pass": false, "weight": 0.20, "reasoning": "No specific next step mentioned in closing" },
   216→    "format": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.10, "reasoning": "Follows expected email structure" },
   217→    "hallucination": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.10, "reasoning": "No fabricated entities or claims" }
   218→  },
   219→  "overall_score": 0.80,
   220→  "label": "PASS"
   221→}
   222→```
   223→
   224→**Dimension selection and weight merging:**
   225→- The agent always evaluates all 6 universal dimensions
   226→- Namespace overrides adjust weights for specific dimensions (e.g., inbox elevates `tone` from 0.10 to 0.15)
   227→- Overridden weights replace universal weights; non-overridden dimensions keep universal weights
   228→- Weights are re-normalized to sum to 1.0 after merging
   229→
   230→**Scoring math:**
   231→- Dimension score: 1.0 if pass, 0.0 if fail (Boolean)
   232→- Overall score: weighted sum using merged rubric weights (always sums to 1.0)
   233→- Label: PASS (≥ 0.70), WARN (0.50-0.70), FAIL (< 0.50)
   234→
   235→**Error handling:**
   236→- Agent timeout (30s): the `exec_log` row exists without corresponding `eval_results` rows. The health dashboard treats missing eval data as "not evaluated" — it does not inflate scores. EWMA remains unchanged (no update on missing data).
   237→- SQLite busy/locked: `db.mjs` uses `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000` (5s retry). If still locked, the write is silently dropped — the exec_log row remains, eval results are lost for this execution only. This is acceptable at 10 evals/day.
   238→
   239→**Cost:** ~$0.0002 per Tier 2 eval on Haiku. At 50 daily executions with 20% sampling = 10 evals/day = **~$0.06/month**. Dramatically cheaper than the research's Sonnet estimate of $2.63/month.
   240→
   241→### Component 3: Hook Integration
   242→
   243→**post-task hook configuration:**
   244→
   245→The existing intelligence engine hooks infrastructure gains an eval step. The hook:
   246→1. Captures command output (writes to temp file)
   247→2. Calls `node eval-runner.mjs` with execution metadata
   248→3. Runner returns sampling decision
   249→4. If Tier 2 needed: hook spawns eval-judge agent in background
   250→
   251→The hook is **non-blocking** — Tier 0+1 complete in <50ms, agent spawns asynchronously, the founder's session continues immediately.
   252→
   253→**pre-task hook configuration:**
   254→
   255→Before each command execution, the hook:
   256→1. Calls `node eval-runner.mjs --check-regression --namespace {ns}`
   257→2. Runner queries latest EWMA scores from `eval_results` (~1ms SQLite query)
   258→3. If EWMA < 0.65: prints warning to stdout
   259→4. Warning shown only once per session per namespace — tracked via `exec_log`: the runner queries `SELECT MAX(executed_at) FROM exec_log WHERE namespace = ? AND t1_details LIKE '%regression_warned%'`. If a warning was already issued within the current Claude Code process lifetime (approximated as last 30 minutes), skip. This avoids temp file management and session boundary ambiguity.
   260→
   261→**EWMA computation:**
   262→
   263→Updated incrementally on each new eval result:
   264→```
   265→ewma_new = 0.15 × score_new + 0.85 × ewma_previous
   266→```
   267→
   268→- α = 0.15 gives a half-life of ~4.3 observations
   269→- Sensitive enough to detect sustained drops within 5-7 executions
   270→- Regression threshold: 0.65 (early warning, before quality becomes noticeable)
   271→- **Initialization:** First eval for a namespace seeds EWMA at the raw score value (no prior to blend with)
   272→- **Ordering:** EWMA is computed by the eval-judge agent at write time using the latest stored EWMA for that namespace+dimension. WAL mode + `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000` serializes concurrent writes. At 10 evals/day, race conditions are negligible.
   273→
   274→### Component 4: Database Schema
   275→
   276→All tables extend `intelligence.db`. Auto-created on first eval run via migration check in `db.mjs`.
   277→
   278→```sql
   279→PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
   280→PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
   281→PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;  -- 5s retry on busy (background agent writes)
   282→
   283→-- Reference data: what quality axes exist
   284→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_dimensions (
   285→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   286→    name        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   287→    description TEXT,
   288→    data_type   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BOOLEAN',  -- BOOLEAN | NUMERIC (future)
   289→    weight      REAL DEFAULT 1.0,
   290→    created_at  TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   291→);
   292→
   293→INSERT OR IGNORE INTO eval_dimensions (id, name, description, weight) VALUES
   294→('dim_completeness',  'completeness',  'All required elements present',      0.20),
   295→('dim_accuracy',      'accuracy',      'Facts, numbers, references correct', 0.25),
   296→('dim_tone',          'tone',          'Appropriate voice and register',     0.10),
   297→('dim_actionability', 'actionability', 'User can act immediately',           0.20),
   298→('dim_format',        'format',        'Follows expected output structure',  0.10),
   299→('dim_hallucination', 'hallucination', 'No fabricated information',          0.15);
   300→
   301→-- Versioned rubric definitions (immutable — archive, never edit)
   302→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_rubrics (
   303→    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   304→    dimension_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   305→    namespace       TEXT,
   306→    command         TEXT,
   307→    version         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   308→    checklist_items TEXT NOT NULL,
   309→    eval_type       TEXT DEFAULT 'llm_judge',
   310→    is_active       INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   311→    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   312→    FOREIGN KEY (dimension_id) REFERENCES eval_dimensions(id)
   313→);
   314→
   315→-- One row per command execution (Tier 0 + Tier 1)
   316→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exec_log (
   317→    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   318→    namespace       TEXT NOT NULL,
   319→    command         TEXT NOT NULL,
   320→    input_hash      TEXT,
   321→    output_hash     TEXT,
   322→    output_preview  TEXT,
   323→    token_input     INTEGER,
   324→    token_output    INTEGER,
   325→    duration_ms     INTEGER,
   326→    t1_pass         INTEGER,
   327→    t1_details      TEXT,
   328→    executed_at     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch())
   329→);
   330→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_exec_log_cmd
   331→    ON exec_log(namespace, command, executed_at);
   332→
   333→-- One row per dimension per Tier 2+ evaluation
   334→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_results (
   335→    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   336→    execution_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   337→    namespace       TEXT NOT NULL,
   338→    command         TEXT NOT NULL,
   339→    dimension_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   340→    rubric_id       TEXT,
   341→    score           REAL NOT NULL,
   342→    pass            INTEGER NOT NULL,
   343→    score_label     TEXT,
   344→    eval_tier       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
   345→    reasoning       TEXT,
   346→    ewma_score      REAL,
   347→    evaluated_at    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
   348→    FOREIGN KEY (execution_id) REFERENCES exec_log(id),
   349→    FOREIGN KEY (dimension_id) REFERENCES eval_dimensions(id)
   350→);
   351→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_results_exec
   352→    ON eval_res

<result>Now I have a complete picture. Let me compile my findings.

---

## Code Review: Implementation Plan vs. Design Spec

**Reviewing:** `/founderOS/docs/superpowers/plans/[9]-2026-03-12-evals-framework.md` against `/founderOS/docs/superpowers/specs/[9]-2026-03-12-evals-framework-design.md`

---

## CRITICAL Issues

### 1. `node -e` with ESM imports will fail at runtime (eval-judge agent write step)

**Confidence: 95**

**File:** Plan, Task 11 (eval-judge.md), Step 4 — `agents/intel/eval-judge.md`

The plan instructs the eval-judge agent to write results using:

```bash
node -e "
import { openDb, insertEvalResult } from './_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/db.mjs';
...
"
```

`node -e` does not support top-level `import` statements from ESM modules. This is a Node.js restriction — inline scripts via `-e` are treated as CommonJS by default (or require `--input-type=module` with piped input via `--input-type`, not `-e`). This will throw `SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement in an eval`. Since the `db.mjs` file uses ESM (`import Database from 'better-sqlite3'`), there is no CommonJS fallback.

**Fix:** The agent should invoke a small wrapper script instead of an inline `-e` expression, e.g.:

```bash
node ${CLAUDE_PLUGIN_ROOT}/_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/write-eval-result.mjs \
  --execution-id EXECUTION_ID --namespace NAMESPACE ...
```

Or the `db.mjs` module should export a CLI entry point callable as a script. The spec describes this as `node db.mjs --write-eval`, but the plan never creates that CLI interface on `db.mjs`.

---

### 2. `db.mjs` missing the `--write-eval` CLI entry point the spec requires

**Confidence: 92**

**File:** Plan, Task 2 (`db.mjs`) / Spec, Component 2 (Agent behavior)

The spec explicitly states:

> "It receives the database path as part of its spawn context and calls the eval runner's `db.mjs` module to INSERT rows into `eval_results` and update the EWMA. **The agent calls `node db.mjs --write-eval` as its final step.**"

The plan's `db.mjs` exports only ES module functions — it has no `if (process.argv[1] === fileURLToPath(import.meta.url))` entry point that would allow it to be invoked as a CLI script with `node db.mjs --write-eval`. The eval-judge agent cannot call it directly as the spec mandates.

**Fix:** Add a CLI entry block to the bottom of `db.mjs` that parses `--write-eval` arguments and calls `insertEvalResult`.

---

### 3. `mergeRubrics` does not re-normalize when only a subset of weights are overridden by the namespace

**Confidence: 85**

**File:** Plan, Task 10 (`eval-runner.mjs`), lines 1040-1050

The merge logic spreads override weights on top of universal weights and then re-normalizes the total. This is correct in isolation, but the spec's rubric format for `inbox.json` and `prep.json` only specifies weights for 2 of 6 dimensions:

```json
"weights": { "tone": 0.15, "actionability": 0.20 }
```

After merging, the six dimensions will have these raw weights: `completeness: 0.20, accuracy: 0.25, tone: 0.15, actionability: 0.20, format: 0.10, hallucination: 0.15` — sum = 1.05. Re-normalization will slightly adjust all weights. This is the intended behavior per the spec ("Override weights replace universal weights for specified dimensions; unspecified dimensions keep universal weights; all weights are re-normalized to sum to 1.0"). The logic is correct.

However, the `invoice.json` override specifies only 4 dimensions in its weights (`accuracy`, `completeness`, `format`, `hallucination`) but sets `sample_rate: 0.0`. After merging, `tone` and `actionability` will still appear in the merged rubric with their universal weights, and the total will be re-normalized. When `sample_rate` is 0.0, `shouldSample` will return `false` for T1-passing executions — but for T1-**failing** invoice runs, `shouldSample` always returns `true` (line 675: `if (!t1Pass) return true`). This means a failed invoice will still be sent to the Tier 2 LLM judge even though the spec says invoices should be **deterministic-only**.

**Fix:** `shouldSample` should additionally check if the merged rubric's `sample_rate` is 0.0 and return `false` unconditionally in that case, rather than only checking this when T1 passes:

```javascript
export function shouldSample(t1Pass, rubric) {
  const sampleRate = rubric?.tier2?.sample_rate ?? 0.20;
  if (sampleRate === 0.0) return false;  // deterministic-only namespaces
  if (!t1Pass) return true;
  return Math.random() < sampleRate;
}
```

---

## MAJOR Issues

### 4. `package.json` location mismatch — `better-sqlite3` added to `scripts/package.json` not the plugin root

**Confidence: 88**

**File:** Plan, Task 3 (Modified Files table + Task 3 step 2) vs. Spec, Component 1

The plan's Modified Files table says: `scripts/package.json | Add better-sqlite3 dependency`

However, the spec says: "**Dependencies:** `better-sqlite3` (added to plugin `package.json`)" — meaning the root plugin manifest package.json, not the scripts utility package.

The current `scripts/package.json` is for the Notion CLI tool and other scripts (`@notionhq/client`). The eval runner lives at `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/eval-runner.mjs` — it is not a script in `scripts/`. When the eval runner runs via `node eval-runner.mjs` from the plugin hooks, Node.js will resolve `better-sqlite3` from the directory containing `node_modules`. If the dependency is only in `scripts/package.json`, the eval runner will fail to import `better-sqlite3` unless `npm install` is run in `scripts/` and the hook explicitly uses `scripts/node_modules`. There is no evidence of a plugin-root `package.json` in the repo, which should be flagged separately — but at minimum this placement is inconsistent with the spec.

**Fix:** Clarify whether a plugin-root `package.json` exists or needs to be created. The plan should explicitly address this rather than silently targeting `scripts/package.json`.

---

### 5. EWMA regression check uses per-namespace EWMA but `insertEvalResult` computes per-namespace EWMA (not per-dimension)

**Confidence: 87**

**File:** Plan, Task 2 (`db.mjs`), `insertEvalResult` and `getLatestEwma` functions (lines 283-313)

`insertEvalResult` computes EWMA by querying:

```sql
SELECT ewma_score FROM eval_results
WHERE namespace = ? AND ewma_score IS NOT NULL
ORDER BY evaluated_at DESC LIMIT 1
```

This fetches the most recent EWMA row across **all dimensions** for a namespace. But each eval produces 6 dimension rows inserted sequentially. The second dimension's EWMA will blend with the first dimension's just-inserted row (from the same eval run), not with the previous eval's EWMA for that dimension. This means dimension EWMAs are contaminated by each other and the overall EWMA trajectory is incoherent.

The spec implies EWMA should be tracked **per namespace** as an aggregate (the regression check references a single EWMA per namespace), but the `eval_results` table stores one row per dimension — so the design requires either a separate EWMA table or computing EWMA over the per-eval aggregated scores, not per-dimension rows. The plan's implementation will produce nonsensical EWMA values.

**Fix:** Either (a) compute EWMA from per-eval overall scores (not per-dimension rows), stored in a separate column on `exec_log`, or (b) scope the EWMA query to `WHERE namespace = ? AND dimension_id = ?` if per-dimension EWMA is intended.

---

### 6. Health command cold start behavior — "0 evals" check happens before database existence check

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 13 (`commands/intel/health.md`), Steps 1–2

Step 1 checks if the database file exists and shows the cold start message if not. Step 2 then issues `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT execution_id) as eval_count FROM eval_results` — but if the database exists but the `eval_results` table doesn't yet (because no evals have run and `ensureSchema` has not been triggered by the runner), this query will throw `SQLite error: no such table: eval_results`. The plan does not handle this error case.

The spec says: "Auto-created on first eval run via migration check in `db.mjs`" — meaning the tables may not exist on a fresh install when `health.md` is first run.

**Fix:** Wrap the `eval_results` query in error handling and treat a missing-table error the same as 0 evals.

---

### 7. `db.mjs` `ensureSchema` is called redundantly — it reads and re-runs DDL from inline strings, ignoring the .sql file it also references

**Confidence: 82**

**File:** Plan, Task 2 (`db.mjs`), lines 195-259

`db.mjs` defines `SCHEMA_PATH` pointing to `intelligence.sql`, but `ensureSchema` never reads that file — it instead re-declares the full DDL inline as a template string. This creates a maintenance split: if the SQL schema is updated in `intelligence.sql`, the inline copy in `db.mjs` will not reflect that change. The import of `readFileSync` at line 192 is never used (it is used only in `openDb` for `mkdirSync`, not `readFileSync`). Additionally, the `readFileSync` import is declared but serves no purpose in the module.

**Fix:** Either remove `SCHEMA_PATH` and the unused `readFileSync` import (if the inline DDL is the source of truth), or update `ensureSchema` to execute the DDL from the SQL file. Given that `intelligence.sql` is a modified file in the plan, keeping them in sync is a real risk.

---

## MINOR Issues

### 8. Spec has 3 indexes on `eval_results`; plan SQL matches — but `eval_dimensions` weights don't add up to 1.0

**Confidence: 85**

**File:** Spec and Plan SQL, `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO eval_dimensions` statements

Both the spec and plan insert these weights: `0.20 + 0.25 + 0.10 + 0.20 + 0.10 + 0.15 = 1.00`. This is correct. However, the spec text for `eval_dimensions` has `dim_hallucination` with weight `0.15`, while the spec's output format JSON (Component 2, scoring section) shows `"hallucination": { "weight": 0.10 }`. There is a minor inconsistency between the table seed data (0.15) and the example JSON output (0.10). The plan uses 0.15 everywhere, which matches the table and universal.json — that is the right choice — but the spec example JSON is misleading.

This is a **spec inconsistency** the plan inherited. The plan's implementation (0.15) is consistent with itself, and no action is needed in the plan, but the spec's example JSON should be noted as incorrect.

---

### 9. `eval-runner.mjs` is declared `async function main()` but `handlePostTaskEval` and `handleRegressionCheck` are synchronous

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 10 (`eval-runner.mjs`), lines 897-909

`main()` is declared `async` and awaited with `.catch()`, but neither `handlePostTaskEval` nor `handleRegressionCheck` are async — they use only synchronous `better-sqlite3` calls (which is intentional and correct). The `async` declaration is harmless but misleading and creates the false impression that await points exist. This is a style issue but could confuse implementers into thinking there is async I/O to handle.

---

### 10. `node --check` validation in Task 15 Step 4 will not work for ESM modules that import from `better-sqlite3`

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 15, Step 4

`node --check` performs syntax checking only and does not resolve or validate imports. However, the bigger concern is that `node --check db.mjs` will pass even if `better-sqlite3` is not installed. The smoke test in Step 6 actually executes the runner with no eval data, which is a real execution test. But `node --check` on ESM files with bare specifier imports (`import Database from 'better-sqlite3'`) does not verify the native module exists. The plan would benefit from noting that the smoke test (Step 6) is the real validation, not `--check`.

---

## Spec Coverage Summary

All Phase 1 deliverables from the spec are addressed in the plan:

| Phase 1 Item | Covered? |
|---|---|
| Node.js eval runner (Tier 0 + Tier 1) | Yes — Tasks 4, 5, 6, 10 |
| Eval judge agent (Haiku, background) | Yes — Task 11 |
| 4-table SQLite schema | Yes — Task 1 |
| Hook integration (post-task + pre-task) | Yes — Task 12 |
| Universal rubric + 3 overrides (inbox, invoice, prep) | Yes — Tasks 8, 9 |
| `/founder-os:intel:health` command | Yes — Task 13 |
| Regression alerting via pre-task hook | Yes — Task 12 |
| `better-sqlite3` added to `package.json` | Yes — Task 3 (but wrong package.json — see Issue 4) |
| `sampler.mjs` | Yes — Task 7 |
| `schema.mjs` (Tier 1 JSON validation) | Yes — Task 6 |

No Phase 1 items are missing from the plan.

---

## Priority Summary

| # | Severity | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | CRITICAL | `node -e` with ESM `import` will fail — agent cannot write results |
| 2 | CRITICAL | `db.mjs` missing `--write-eval` CLI entry point required by spec and agent |
| 3 | CRITICAL | `shouldSample` always returns `true` on T1 fail, bypassing `sample_rate: 0.0` for invoice namespace |
| 4 | MAJOR | `better-sqlite3` added to wrong `package.json` (`scripts/` not plugin root) |
| 5 | MAJOR | EWMA computation is per-namespace across all dimension rows, not per-eval aggregate — produces incoherent values |
| 6 | MAJOR | Health command will crash on missing `eval_results` table with no error handling |
| 7 | MAJOR | `db.mjs` has unused `readFileSync` import; schema DDL is duplicated and will drift from `intelligence.sql` |
| 8 | MINOR | Spec example JSON shows `hallucination` weight as 0.10 but table/rubric uses 0.15 — spec inconsistency |
| 9 | MINOR | `main()` declared `async` unnecessarily |
| 10 | MINOR | `node --check` does not validate native module availability |</result>

{"parentUuid":"e7200379-6f78-4107-ab7d-d0c0ad40416f","isSidechain":true,"promptId":"816adb34-9916-4b87-bdb8-a5cdf8441b9c","agentId":"ac5270b7f24ffa85e","type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"REDACTED","type":"tool_result","content":"     1→# Evals & Quality Framework — Design Spec
     2→
     3→**Date**: 2026-03-12
     4→**Status**: Draft
     5→**Priority**: P2 — Quality infrastructure
     6→**Research**: `docs/specs/evals-research.md`
     7→
     8→## Problem
     9→
    10→Founder OS has 32 command namespaces producing LLM-generated outputs (email drafts, meeting briefs, invoices, reports). There is no systematic way to measure output quality, detect regressions, or feed quality signals back into the learning cycle. The existing intelligence engine tracks pattern confidence but doesn't evaluate whether outputs actually satisfy the founder's needs.
    11→
    12→Without quality measurement, the system can't answer: "Is my AI assistant getting better or worse?"
    13→
    14→## Solution
    15→
    16→A three-layer eval framework embedded as a sub-module of the existing intelligence engine. Deterministic checks run synchronously via a Node.js runner. LLM-based scoring runs asynchronously via a background Haiku agent. Results feed into the existing pattern lifecycle. Founders see nothing unless quality regresses — then they get a warning.
    17→
    18→**Key design principles:**
    19→- Zero founder configuration — evals work automatically after plugin install
    20→- Zero session overhead — LLM scoring runs in background, never blocks the founder
    21→- Programmatic where possible — Tier 0 and Tier 1 are pure Node.js, not LLM-interpreted
    22→- Silent by default — regression alerts only, no inline badges or scores
    23→- Minimal schema — 4 tables, add more only when proven necessary
    24→
    25→## Design
    26→
    27→### Architecture Overview
    28→
    29→```
    30→EVAL PIPELINE
    31→=============
    32→
    33→Founder runs /founder-os:namespace:command
    34→                    │
    35→                    ▼
    36→         ┌──────────────────┐
    37→         │  COMMAND EXECUTES │
    38→         │  (normal flow)    │
    39→         └────────┬─────────┘
    40→                  │ output generated
    41→                  ▼
    42→         ┌──────────────────┐
    43→         │  post-task HOOK   │
    44→         │  fires            │
    45→         └────────┬─────────┘
    46→                  │
    47→                  ▼
    48→    ┌─────────────────────────────┐
    49→    │  NODE.JS EVAL RUNNER        │
    50→    │  (synchronous, <50ms)       │
    51→    │                             │
    52→    │  Tier 0: Telemetry          │
    53→    │  • Token count, duration    │
    54→    │  • SHA-256 input/output     │
    55→    │  • INSERT into exec_log     │
    56→    │                             │
    57→    │  Tier 1: Format Checks      │
    58→    │  • Required sections        │
    59→    │  • Word count bounds        │
    60→    │  • Forbidden patterns       │
    61→    │  • UPDATE exec_log.t1_pass  │
    62→    │                             │
    63→    │  Sampling Decision          │
    64→    │  • T1 fail → always Tier 2  │
    65→    │  • Otherwise → 20% random   │
    66→    └──────────┬──────────────────┘
    67→               │
    68→        ┌──────┴──────┐
    69→   NOT SAMPLED     SAMPLED
    70→        │              │
    71→        ▼              ▼
    72→   exit 0          SPAWN eval-judge
    73→   (done)          AGENT (background)
    74→                   model: haiku
    75→                        │
    76→                        ▼
    77→                   ┌─────────────────┐
    78→                   │  EVAL JUDGE      │
    79→                   │  (async, ~500ms) │
    80→                   │                  │
    81→                   │  Per dimension:  │
    82→                   │  • Read criterion│
    83→                   │  • YES/NO judge  │
    84→                   │  • 1-line reason │
    85→                   │                  │
    86→                   │  Write results   │
    87→                   │  to eval_results │
    88→                   │  Update EWMA     │
    89→                   └─────────────────┘
    90→
    91→REGRESSION ALERTING (separate flow)
    92→====================================
    93→
    94→Founder runs any command
    95→        │
    96→        ▼
    97→   pre-task HOOK fires
    98→        │
    99→        ▼
   100→   node eval-runner.mjs --check-regression
   101→        │
   102→        ▼
   103→   Query EWMA for namespace (~1ms)
   104→        │
   105→   ├── EWMA ≥ 0.65 → silent (exit 0)
   106→   └── EWMA < 0.65 → print warning:
   107→       "⚠️ Quality for {ns} commands has dropped
   108→        recently. Run /founder-os:intel:health"
   109→       (once per session per namespace)
   110→```
   111→
   112→### Component 1: Deterministic Runner
   113→
   114→**Location:** `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/`
   115→
   116→```
   117→_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/
   118→├── eval-runner.mjs          — Main entry, called by post-task hook
   119→├── checks/
   120→│   ├── telemetry.mjs        — Tier 0: token count, duration, hashes
   121→│   ├── format.mjs           — Tier 1: sections, word count, forbidden patterns
   122→│   └── schema.mjs           — Tier 1: JSON/structure validation
   123→├── rubrics/
   124→│   ├── universal.json       — Default 6-dimension rubric
   125→│   └── overrides/           — Per-namespace rubric overrides
   126→│       ├── inbox.json
   127→│       ├── invoice.json
   128→│       ├── prep.json
   129→│       └── (added per namespace as needed)
   130→├── db.mjs                   — SQLite read/write, migration checks
   131→└── sampler.mjs              — Sampling decision logic
   132→```
   133→
   134→**Entry point:** `eval-runner.mjs` accepts CLI arguments:
   135→
   136→```bash
   137→# Post-task eval mode
   138→node eval-runner.mjs \
   139→  --namespace inbox \
   140→  --command triage \
   141→  --output-file /tmp/fos-exec-{id}.txt \
   142→  --tokens-in 1200 \
   143→  --tokens-out 800 \
   144→  --duration-ms 3400
   145→
   146→# Regression check mode
   147→node eval-runner.mjs --check-regression --namespace inbox
   148→```
   149→
   150→**Rubric format (JSON, not markdown):**
   151→
   152→```json
   153→{
   154→  "namespace": "inbox",
   155→  "tier1": {
   156→    "required_sections": ["Subject:", "Body:"],
   157→    "min_words": 50,
   158→    "max_words": 500,
   159→    "forbidden_patterns": ["[PLACEHOLDER]", "INSERT_NAME", "{name}"],
   160→    "required_json_fields": null
   161→  },
   162→  "tier2": {
   163→    "sample_rate": 0.20,
   164→    "dimensions": ["accuracy", "tone", "actionability"],
   165→    "weights": {
   166→      "accuracy": 0.25,
   167→      "tone": 0.15,
   168→      "actionability": 0.20
   169→    },
   170→    "criteria": {
   171→      "accuracy": "No fabricated data or claims not present in source",
   172→      "tone": "Professional, matches founder voice profile",
   173→      "actionability": "Includes concrete next step the founder can take"
   174→    },
   175→    "threshold": 0.70
   176→  }
   177→}
   178→```
   179→
   180→**Rubric merging rules:**
   181→- Namespace overrides merge on top of `universal.json`
   182→- If no override exists for a namespace, universal rubric applies (both Tier 1 and Tier 2)
   183→- `universal.json` defines baseline Tier 1 checks (non-empty output, min 10 words, max 5000 words) and all 6 Tier 2 dimensions with default weights
   184→- Namespace overrides can: add Tier 1 checks (e.g., required sections), adjust Tier 2 dimension weights, override criteria text, change sample rate
   185→- Override weights replace universal weights for specified dimensions; unspecified dimensions keep universal weights; all weights are re-normalized to sum to 1.0
   186→
   187→**Dependencies:** `better-sqlite3` (added to plugin `package.json`), `crypto` (built-in Node.js). No other runtime dependencies.
   188→
   189→### Component 2: Eval Judge Agent
   190→
   191→**Location:** `agents/intel/eval-judge.md`
   192→
   193→**Configuration:**
   194→- Model: `haiku` (Boolean rubric scoring is low-complexity)
   195→- Execution: `run_in_background: true` (non-blocking)
   196→- Timeout: 30 seconds
   197→
   198→**Agent behavior:**
   199→1. Receives: command output text, rubric criteria, execution context, and the `intelligence.db` file path
   200→2. For each dimension in the rubric: evaluates YES/NO with one-sentence reasoning
   201→3. Computes weighted overall score across all evaluated dimensions
   202→4. Writes results directly to `eval_results` table via `db.mjs` (the agent calls `node db.mjs --write-eval` as its final step)
   203→
   204→**Write path:** The agent is responsible for persisting its own results. It receives the database path as part of its spawn context and calls the eval runner's `db.mjs` module to INSERT rows into `eval_results` and update the EWMA. This avoids the anti-pattern of the hook trying to collect results from an async process it no longer controls.
   205→
   206→**Output format (all 6 universal dimensions evaluated, namespace overrides adjust weights):**
   207→
   208→```json
   209→{
   210→  "execution_id": "exec-abc123",
   211→  "scores": {
   212→    "completeness": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.20, "reasoning": "All requested sections present" },
   213→    "accuracy": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.25, "reasoning": "All claims supported by source email data" },
   214→    "tone": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.15, "reasoning": "Professional register, appropriate for client context" },
   215→    "actionability": { "pass": false, "weight": 0.20, "reasoning": "No specific next step mentioned in closing" },
   216→    "format": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.10, "reasoning": "Follows expected email structure" },
   217→    "hallucination": { "pass": true, "weight": 0.10, "reasoning": "No fabricated entities or claims" }
   218→  },
   219→  "overall_score": 0.80,
   220→  "label": "PASS"
   221→}
   222→```
   223→
   224→**Dimension selection and weight merging:**
   225→- The agent always evaluates all 6 universal dimensions
   226→- Namespace overrides adjust weights for specific dimensions (e.g., inbox elevates `tone` from 0.10 to 0.15)
   227→- Overridden weights replace universal weights; non-overridden dimensions keep universal weights
   228→- Weights are re-normalized to sum to 1.0 after merging
   229→
   230→**Scoring math:**
   231→- Dimension score: 1.0 if pass, 0.0 if fail (Boolean)
   232→- Overall score: weighted sum using merged rubric weights (always sums to 1.0)
   233→- Label: PASS (≥ 0.70), WARN (0.50-0.70), FAIL (< 0.50)
   234→
   235→**Error handling:**
   236→- Agent timeout (30s): the `exec_log` row exists without corresponding `eval_results` rows. The health dashboard treats missing eval data as "not evaluated" — it does not inflate scores. EWMA remains unchanged (no update on missing data).
   237→- SQLite busy/locked: `db.mjs` uses `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000` (5s retry). If still locked, the write is silently dropped — the exec_log row remains, eval results are lost for this execution only. This is acceptable at 10 evals/day.
   238→
   239→**Cost:** ~$0.0002 per Tier 2 eval on Haiku. At 50 daily executions with 20% sampling = 10 evals/day = **~$0.06/month**. Dramatically cheaper than the research's Sonnet estimate of $2.63/month.
   240→
   241→### Component 3: Hook Integration
   242→
   243→**post-task hook configuration:**
   244→
   245→The existing intelligence engine hooks infrastructure gains an eval step. The hook:
   246→1. Captures command output (writes to temp file)
   247→2. Calls `node eval-runner.mjs` with execution metadata
   248→3. Runner returns sampling decision
   249→4. If Tier 2 needed: hook spawns eval-judge agent in background
   250→
   251→The hook is **non-blocking** — Tier 0+1 complete in <50ms, agent spawns asynchronously, the founder's session continues immediately.
   252→
   253→**pre-task hook configuration:**
   254→
   255→Before each command execution, the hook:
   256→1. Calls `node eval-runner.mjs --check-regression --namespace {ns}`
   257→2. Runner queries latest EWMA scores from `eval_results` (~1ms SQLite query)
   258→3. If EWMA < 0.65: prints warning to stdout
   259→4. Warning shown only once per session per namespace — tracked via `exec_log`: the runner queries `SELECT MAX(executed_at) FROM exec_log WHERE namespace = ? AND t1_details LIKE '%regression_warned%'`. If a warning was already issued within the current Claude Code process lifetime (approximated as last 30 minutes), skip. This avoids temp file management and session boundary ambiguity.
   260→
   261→**EWMA computation:**
   262→
   263→Updated incrementally on each new eval result:
   264→```
   265→ewma_new = 0.15 × score_new + 0.85 × ewma_previous
   266→```
   267→
   268→- α = 0.15 gives a half-life of ~4.3 observations
   269→- Sensitive enough to detect sustained drops within 5-7 executions
   270→- Regression threshold: 0.65 (early warning, before quality becomes noticeable)
   271→- **Initialization:** First eval for a namespace seeds EWMA at the raw score value (no prior to blend with)
   272→- **Ordering:** EWMA is computed by the eval-judge agent at write time using the latest stored EWMA for that namespace+dimension. WAL mode + `PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000` serializes concurrent writes. At 10 evals/day, race conditions are negligible.
   273→
   274→### Component 4: Database Schema
   275→
   276→All tables extend `intelligence.db`. Auto-created on first eval run via migration check in `db.mjs`.
   277→
   278→```sql
   279→PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
   280→PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
   281→PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;  -- 5s retry on busy (background agent writes)
   282→
   283→-- Reference data: what quality axes exist
   284→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_dimensions (
   285→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   286→    name        TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
   287→    description TEXT,
   288→    data_type   TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'BOOLEAN',  -- BOOLEAN | NUMERIC (future)
   289→    weight      REAL DEFAULT 1.0,
   290→    created_at  TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   291→);
   292→
   293→INSERT OR IGNORE INTO eval_dimensions (id, name, description, weight) VALUES
   294→('dim_completeness',  'completeness',  'All required elements present',      0.20),
   295→('dim_accuracy',      'accuracy',      'Facts, numbers, references correct', 0.25),
   296→('dim_tone',          'tone',          'Appropriate voice and register',     0.10),
   297→('dim_actionability', 'actionability', 'User can act immediately',           0.20),
   298→('dim_format',        'format',        'Follows expected output structure',  0.10),
   299→('dim_hallucination', 'hallucination', 'No fabricated information',          0.15);
   300→
   301→-- Versioned rubric definitions (immutable — archive, never edit)
   302→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_rubrics (
   303→    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   304→    dimension_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   305→    namespace       TEXT,
   306→    command         TEXT,
   307→    version         INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 1,
   308→    checklist_items TEXT NOT NULL,
   309→    eval_type       TEXT DEFAULT 'llm_judge',
   310→    is_active       INTEGER DEFAULT 1,
   311→    created_at      TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   312→    FOREIGN KEY (dimension_id) REFERENCES eval_dimensions(id)
   313→);
   314→
   315→-- One row per command execution (Tier 0 + Tier 1)
   316→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS exec_log (
   317→    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   318→    namespace       TEXT NOT NULL,
   319→    command         TEXT NOT NULL,
   320→    input_hash      TEXT,
   321→    output_hash     TEXT,
   322→    output_preview  TEXT,
   323→    token_input     INTEGER,
   324→    token_output    INTEGER,
   325→    duration_ms     INTEGER,
   326→    t1_pass         INTEGER,
   327→    t1_details      TEXT,
   328→    executed_at     INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch())
   329→);
   330→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_exec_log_cmd
   331→    ON exec_log(namespace, command, executed_at);
   332→
   333→-- One row per dimension per Tier 2+ evaluation
   334→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS eval_results (
   335→    id              TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   336→    execution_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   337→    namespace       TEXT NOT NULL,
   338→    command         TEXT NOT NULL,
   339→    dimension_id    TEXT NOT NULL,
   340→    rubric_id       TEXT,
   341→    score           REAL NOT NULL,
   342→    pass            INTEGER NOT NULL,
   343→    score_label     TEXT,
   344→    eval_tier       INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
   345→    reasoning       TEXT,
   346→    ewma_score      REAL,
   347→    evaluated_at    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT (unixepoch()),
   348→    FOREIGN KEY (execution_id) REFERENCES exec_log(id),
   349→    FOREIGN KEY (dimension_id) REFERENCES eval_dimensions(id)
   350→);
   351→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_results_exec
   352→    ON eval_results(execution_id);
   353→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_results_ns_time
   354→    ON eval_results(namespace, command, evaluated_at);
   355→-- Covering index for regression check hot path (pre-task hook, every command)
   356→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_eval_results_ewma
   357→    ON eval_results(namespace, evaluated_at DESC, ewma_score);
   358→```
   359→
   360→**Column naming convention:** The existing intelligence engine tables (`events`, `patterns`, `healing_patterns`) use the column name `plugin` where these eval tables use `namespace`. Both refer to the same concept (the 32 Founder OS command namespaces). The eval tables use `namespace` for clarity since "plugin" is ambiguous in a single-plugin architecture. When joining across boundaries (e.g., `eval_results` with `patterns`), use `eval_results.namespace = patterns.plugin`.
   361→
   362→**Storage estimate:** ~300 bytes per `eval_results` row. At 10 evals/day × 365 days = 3,650 rows/year (~1.1 MB). With `exec_log` at 50 rows/day = 18,250 rows/year (~3.6 MB). Total eval storage: **< 5 MB/year**. SQLite handles this trivially.
   363→
   364→**Retention:** Raw data kept 90 days. Cleanup triggered by the eval runner itself: on every 100th `exec_log` INSERT (checked via `exec_log` row count modulo 100), the runner runs `DELETE FROM eval_results WHERE evaluated_at < unixepoch() - 7776000` and same for `exec_log`. This piggybacks on existing execution flow — no cron, no daemon, no separate trigger needed. At 50 executions/day, cleanup runs roughly twice daily.
   365→
   366→### Component 5: User-Facing Command
   367→
   368→**Phase 1 — Single command:** `/founder-os:intel:health`
   369→
   370→**Location:*

content.text: Let me get just the review findings:

content.content:
**Fix:** Either (a) compute EWMA from per-eval overall scores (not per-dimension rows), stored in a separate column on `exec_log`, or (b) scope the EWMA query to `WHERE namespace = ? AND dimension_id = ?` if per-dimension EWMA is intended.

---

### 6. Health command cold start behavior — "0 evals" check happens before database existence check

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 13 (`commands/intel/health.md`), Steps 1–2

Step 1 checks if the database file exists and shows the cold start message if not. Step 2 then issues `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT execution_id) as eval_count FROM eval_results` — but if the database exists but the `eval_results` table doesn't yet (because no evals have run and `ensureSchema` has not been triggered by the runner), this query will throw `SQLite error: no such table: eval_results`. The plan does not handle this error case.

The spec says: "Auto-created on first eval run via migration check in `db.mjs`" — meaning the tables may not exist on a fresh install when `health.md` is first run.

**Fix:** Wrap the `eval_results` query in error handling and treat a missing-table error the same as 0 evals.

---

### 7. `db.mjs` `ensureSchema` is called redundantly — it reads and re-runs DDL from inline strings, ignoring the .sql file it also references

**Confidence: 82**

**File:** Plan, Task 2 (`db.mjs`), lines 195-259

`db.mjs` defines `SCHEMA_PATH` pointing to `intelligence.sql`, but `ensureSchema` never reads that file — it instead re-declares the full DDL inline as a template string. This creates a maintenance split: if the SQL schema is updated in `intelligence.sql`, the inline copy in `db.mjs` will not reflect that change. The import of `readFileSync` at line 192 is never used (it is used only in `openDb` for `mkdirSync`, not `readFileSync`). Additionally, the `readFileSync` import is declared but serves no purpose in the module.

**Fix:** Either remove `SCHEMA_PATH` and the unused `readFileSync` import (if the inline DDL is the source of truth), or update `ensureSchema` to execute the DDL from the SQL file. Given that `intelligence.sql` is a modified file in the plan, keeping them in sync is a real risk.

---

## MINOR Issues

### 8. Spec has 3 indexes on `eval_results`; plan SQL matches — but `eval_dimensions` weights don't add up to 1.0

**Confidence: 85**

**File:** Spec and Plan SQL, `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO eval_dimensions` statements

Both the spec and plan insert these weights: `0.20 + 0.25 + 0.10 + 0.20 + 0.10 + 0.15 = 1.00`. This is correct. However, the spec text for `eval_dimensions` has `dim_hallucination` with weight `0.15`, while the spec's output format JSON (Component 2, scoring section) shows `"hallucination": { "weight": 0.10 }`. There is a minor inconsistency between the table seed data (0.15) and the example JSON output (0.10). The plan uses 0.15 everywhere, which matches the table and universal.json — that is the right choice — but the spec example JSON is misleading.

This is a **spec inconsistency** the plan inherited. The plan's implementation (0.15) is consistent with itself, and no action is needed in the plan, but the spec's example JSON should be noted as incorrect.

---

### 9. `eval-runner.mjs` is declared `async function main()` but `handlePostTaskEval` and `handleRegressionCheck` are synchronous

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 10 (`eval-runner.mjs`), lines 897-909

`main()` is declared `async` and awaited with `.catch()`, but neither `handlePostTaskEval` nor `handleRegressionCheck` are async — they use only synchronous `better-sqlite3` calls (which is intentional and correct). The `async` declaration is harmless but misleading and creates the false impression that await points exist. This is a style issue but could confuse implementers into thinking there is async I/O to handle.

---

### 10. `node --check` validation in Task 15 Step 4 will not work for ESM modules that import from `better-sqlite3`

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 15, Step 4

`node --check` performs syntax checking only and does not resolve or validate imports. However, the bigger concern is that `node --check db.mjs` will pass even if `better-sqlite3` is not installed. The smoke test in Step 6 actually executes the runner with no eval data, which is a real execution test. But `node --check` on ESM files with bare specifier imports (`import Database from 'better-sqlite3'`) does not verify the native module exists. The plan would benefit from noting that the smoke test (Step 6) is the real validation, not `--check`.

---

## Spec Coverage Summary

All Phase 1 deliverables from the spec are addressed in the plan:

| Phase 1 Item | Covered? |
|---|---|

No Phase 1 items are missing from the plan.

---

## Priority Summary

| # | Severity | Issue |
|---|---|---|
| 1 | CRITICAL | `node -e` with ESM `import` will fail — agent cannot write results |
| 2 | CRITICAL | `db.mjs` missing `--write-eval` CLI entry point required by spec and agent |
| 3 | CRITICAL | `shouldSample` always returns `true` on T1 fail, bypassing `sample_rate: 0.0` for invoice namespace |
| 4 | MAJOR | `better-sqlite3` added to wrong `package.json` (`scripts/` not plugin root) |
| 5 | MAJOR | EWMA computation is per-namespace across all dimension rows, not per-eval aggregate — produces incoherent values |
| 6 | MAJOR | Health command will crash on missing `eval_results` table with no error handling |
| 7 | MAJOR | `db.mjs` has unused `readFileSync` import; schema DDL is duplicated and will drift from `intelligence.sql` |
| 8 | MINOR | Spec example JSON shows `hallucination` weight as 0.10 but table/rubric uses 0.15 — spec inconsistency |
| 9 | MINOR | `main()` declared `async` unnecessarily |
| 10 | MINOR | `node --check` does not validate native module availability |

toolUseResult.stdout:
**Fix:** Either (a) compute EWMA from per-eval overall scores (not per-dimension rows), stored in a separate column on `exec_log`, or (b) scope the EWMA query to `WHERE namespace = ? AND dimension_id = ?` if per-dimension EWMA is intended.

---

### 6. Health command cold start behavior — "0 evals" check happens before database existence check

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 13 (`commands/intel/health.md`), Steps 1–2

Step 1 checks if the database file exists and shows the cold start message if not. Step 2 then issues `SELECT COUNT(DISTINCT execution_id) as eval_count FROM eval_results` — but if the database exists but the `eval_results` table doesn't yet (because no evals have run and `ensureSchema` has not been triggered by the runner), this query will throw `SQLite error: no such table: eval_results`. The plan does not handle this error case.

The spec says: "Auto-created on first eval run via migration check in `db.mjs`" — meaning the tables may not exist on a fresh install when `health.md` is first run.

**Fix:** Wrap the `eval_results` query in error handling and treat a missing-table error the same as 0 evals.

---

### 7. `db.mjs` `ensureSchema` is called redundantly — it reads and re-runs DDL from inline strings, ignoring the .sql file it also references

**Confidence: 82**

**File:** Plan, Task 2 (`db.mjs`), lines 195-259

`db.mjs` defines `SCHEMA_PATH` pointing to `intelligence.sql`, but `ensureSchema` never reads that file — it instead re-declares the full DDL inline as a template string. This creates a maintenance split: if the SQL schema is updated in `intelligence.sql`, the inline copy in `db.mjs` will not reflect that change. The import of `readFileSync` at line 192 is never used (it is used only in `openDb` for `mkdirSync`, not `readFileSync`). Additionally, the `readFileSync` import is declared but serves no purpose in the module.

**Fix:** Either remove `SCHEMA_PATH` and the unused `readFileSync` import (if the inline DDL is the source of truth), or update `ensureSchema` to execute the DDL from the SQL file. Given that `intelligence.sql` is a modified file in the plan, keeping them in sync is a real risk.

---

## MINOR Issues

### 8. Spec has 3 indexes on `eval_results`; plan SQL matches — but `eval_dimensions` weights don't add up to 1.0

**Confidence: 85**

**File:** Spec and Plan SQL, `INSERT OR IGNORE INTO eval_dimensions` statements

Both the spec and plan insert these weights: `0.20 + 0.25 + 0.10 + 0.20 + 0.10 + 0.15 = 1.00`. This is correct. However, the spec text for `eval_dimensions` has `dim_hallucination` with weight `0.15`, while the spec's output format JSON (Component 2, scoring section) shows `"hallucination": { "weight": 0.10 }`. There is a minor inconsistency between the table seed data (0.15) and the example JSON output (0.10). The plan uses 0.15 everywhere, which matches the table and universal.json — that is the right choice — but the spec example JSON is misleading.

This is a **spec inconsistency** the plan inherited. The plan's implementation (0.15) is consistent with itself, and no action is needed in the plan, but the spec's example JSON should be noted as incorrect.

---

### 9. `eval-runner.mjs` is declared `async function main()` but `handlePostTaskEval` and `handleRegressionCheck` are synchronous

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 10 (`eval-runner.mjs`), lines 897-909

`main()` is declared `async` and awaited with `.catch()`, but neither `handlePostTaskEval` nor `handleRegressionCheck` are async — they use only synchronous `better-sqlite3` calls (which is intentional and correct). The `async` declaration is harmless but misleading and creates the false impression that await points exist. This is a style issue but could confuse implementers into thinking there is async I/O to handle.

---

### 10. `node --check` validation in Task 15 Step 4 will not work for ESM modules that import from `better-sqlite3`

**Confidence: 80**

**File:** Plan, Task 15, Step 4

`node --check` performs syntax checking only and does not resolve or validate imports. However, the bigger concern is that `node --check db.mjs` will pass even if `better-sqlite3` is not installed. The smoke test in Step 6 actually executes the runner with no eval data, which is a real execution test. But `node --check` on ESM files with bare specifier imports (`import Database from 'better-sqlite3'`) does not verify the native module exists. The plan would benefit from noting that the smoke test (Step 6) is the real validation, not `--check`.

---

## Spec Coverage Summary

|---|---|
| Node.js eval runner (Tier 0 + Tier 1) | Yes — Tasks 4, 5, 6, 10 |

| Universal rubric + 3 overrides (inbox, invoice, prep) | Yes — Tasks 8, 9 |
| `/founder-os:intel:health` command | Yes — Task 13 |
| Regression alerting via pre-task hook | Yes — Task 12 |
| `better-sqlite3` added to `package.json` | Yes — Task 3 (but wrong package.json — see Issue 4) |
| `sampler.mjs` | Yes — Task 7 |
| `schema.mjs` (Tier 1 JSON validation) | Yes — Task 6 |

No Phase 1 items are missing from the plan.

---

## Priority Summary

| 2 | CRITICAL | `db.mjs` missing `--write-eval` CLI entry point required by spec and agent |

# Evals & Quality Framework Implementation Plan

> **For agentic workers:** REQUIRED: Use superpowers:subagent-driven-development (if subagents available) or superpowers:executing-plans to implement this plan. Steps use checkbox (`- [ ]`) syntax for tracking.

**Goal:** Build a three-layer eval pipeline (telemetry, format checks, LLM judge) embedded in the Intelligence Engine that measures command output quality, detects regressions, and surfaces a quality dashboard — all with zero founder configuration.

**Architecture:** Node.js eval runner called by hooks, SQLite tables extending `intelligence.db`, background Haiku judge agent for sampled LLM scoring, EWMA regression detection via pre-task hook. Rubrics are JSON files with universal + namespace overrides. Everything is Phase 1 scope only.

**Tech Stack:** Node.js (ESM), `better-sqlite3` (native SQLite), JSON rubrics, markdown agent/command definitions

**Spec:** `docs/superpowers/specs/[9]-2026-03-12-evals-framework-design.md`

**Dependency:** Intelligence Engine [3] fully complete — hooks, learning tiers 1-3, self-healing, routing, observation blocks in all 82 command files. This plan extends that infrastructure.

---

## File Structure

### New Files (12)

| File | Responsibility |
|------|---------------|
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/db.mjs` | SQLite migration, read/write helpers, EWMA computation |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/eval-runner.mjs` | Main entry point — orchestrates Tier 0 → Tier 1 → sampling → judge spawn |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/checks/telemetry.mjs` | Tier 0: token counts, duration, SHA-256 hashes |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/checks/format.mjs` | Tier 1: required sections, word count bounds, forbidden patterns |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/checks/schema.mjs` | Tier 1: JSON/structure validation |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/sampler.mjs` | Sampling decision: T1 fail → always Tier 2, otherwise 20% random |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/rubrics/universal.json` | Default 6-dimension rubric with Tier 1 + Tier 2 config |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/rubrics/overrides/inbox.json` | Inbox namespace override (elevated tone, email-specific checks) |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/rubrics/overrides/invoice.json` | Invoice namespace override (deterministic-only, elevated accuracy) |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/evals/rubrics/overrides/prep.json` | Prep namespace override (elevated completeness, context quality) |
| `agents/intel/eval-judge.md` | Haiku background agent for Tier 2 Boolean rubric scoring |
| `commands/intel/health.md` | `/founder-os:intel:health` quality dashboard command |

### Modified Files (4)

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/package.json` | Add `better-sqlite3` dependency |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/hooks/schema/intelligence.sql` | Add 4 eval tables + indexes |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/SKILL.md` | Add evals module reference |
| `_infrastructure/intelligence/hooks/SKILL.md` | Add post-task eval trigger + pre-task regression check |

---

## Chunk 1: Database Schema + db.mjs Module

Foundation layer — everything else depends on this.

lines: +    const getArg = (name) => { const i = args.indexOf(name); return i >= 0 ? args[i + 1] : null; };

+**Note on EWMA scope:** The EWMA is computed per-namespace across all dimension rows. This provides a single namespace-level quality signal for regression detection. The `getLatestEwma` function returns the most recent EWMA from any dimension row for the namespace, which gives a rolling aggregate view. For the regression check threshold (0.65), this is appropriate since we want to detect broad quality drops, not per-dimension regressions. Per-dimension analysis is available in the health dashboard via `getHealthData`.
